bead.title: execute-bead: set Pdeathsig on harness children so the kernel kills them when the worker dies abnormally (SIGKILL/crash)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, host reboot of the supervising process) while a bead attempt is in flight, the spawned harness child (e.g. `claude --print -p --verbose --output-format stream-json ... <execute-bead>` or `codex exec --json ...`) is NOT reaped. It reparents to PID 1 and keeps running indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27 on the dev host: a `claude --print` execute-bead child orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent had gone. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanup works, but it cannot run when the parent is killed uncatchably.

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156-157 — OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir spawns the harness via exec.Command(binary, args...) then calls cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd).
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 — cmdSetProcessGroup sets ONLY SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}. There is NO Pdeathsig, so the kernel does nothing automatically when the parent dies.
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:190-194 / :231-255 — cmdKillProcessGroup is only invoked from the executor's graceful paths (idle/wall-clock timeout, auth/rate-limit pattern, ctx cancellation). These never run on an uncatchable parent death.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-50 — the worker context is signal.NotifyContext on os.Interrupt + SIGTERM only; SIGKILL/crash cannot be caught, so the defer/stopProcess chain never fires and the child orphans.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_windows.go:7-8 — cmdSetProcessGroup is a documented no-op on Windows; Pdeathsig is Linux-only, so the non-Linux gap must be handled explicitly.

PROPOSED FIX
Set SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig = SIGKILL in cmdSetProcessGroup (cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go) so the kernel signals the harness child when the parent thread dies, regardless of how the parent died. Handle the well-known caveat that Pdeathsig keys off the spawning OS *thread*: guard against Go runtime thread migration (runtime.LockOSThread around cmd.Start, or re-verify parent PID in the child path) so the death signal actually fires. Document the macOS/Windows gap (Pdeathsig is Linux-only) and the chosen fallback there (the orphan reaper child bead is the cross-platform backstop). Note: Pdeathsig is in golang.org/x/sys/unix / syscall on Linux only; keep the !windows build constraint and any darwin-specific behavior correct.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works (executor.go:231-255); this change must NOT regress it.
- Startup-time reaping of orphans from already-dead prior workers is a separate child (the backstop reaper).
- docker-clone backend process-group cleanup is a separate child.
- The harness-runner-INTERNAL leak is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this bead is strictly the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child spawn boundary.

CROSS-REF: fizeau-50b6f055 (fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup).
bead.acceptance:
1. A harness child spawned by the execute-bead executor does NOT survive abnormal death of its parent worker. TestExecutor_OrphanKilledOnParentSIGKILL starts the executor (or a thin test harness around ExecuteInDir) against a fake harness binary that forks a long-lived grandchild, SIGKILLs the parent worker process (not graceful SIGTERM), and asserts the harness child + grandchild are gone (not PID-1 orphans) within a bounded grace.
2. TestCmdSetProcessGroup_Pdeathsig asserts cmdSetProcessGroup sets SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig (to SIGKILL) on Linux in cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go.
3. The thread-migration caveat is handled (runtime.LockOSThread around Start, or equivalent parent-PID re-check) so the death signal reliably fires; a test or a documented code-comment rationale covers this, and the non-Linux fallback (Windows no-op / macOS gap) is documented with the reaper as the backstop.
4. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown still terminates in-flight harness children (regression guard). TestExecutor_GracefulShutdownKillsChild cancels the executor context / sends graceful stop and asserts the child process group is killed via cmdKillProcessGroup.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
